"Slug" is the SEO-friendly version of the URL. Example /kari-norman
"Slug" is the SEO-friendly version of the URL. Example /my-course
"Slug" is the SEO-friendly version of the URL. Example /oslo
* Note: This section is still under development.
Course design – Create the pages you need for courses, categories, locations and instructors via "WordPress pages". Shortcodes are inserted automatically.

Hide only the category from filter – the category is hidden from filter options, but courses tagged with the category still appear in the course list under their other categories. Useful for "broad" tags that do not make sense as filters (e.g. "Lectures", "HMS and safety courses").

Hide the category and all related courses – both the category and all courses tagged with the category are completely hidden from the course list. Useful for internal categories that should not be visible to visitors.
Show – the category appears in the filter and courses appear in the list (default).

Return to Kursagenten data:

  1. Disable the "Override profile from Kursagenten" button
  2. Update the instructor profile in Kursagenten
  3. The profile is updated here. Note: the instructor must be linked to a course for the data to be fetched.

- If you use data from Kursagenten, you do not need to keep it updated in two places.
- An instructor created in Kursagenten with the same name will automatically be merged with this instructor.

Use the Kursagenten instructor profile instead:

  1. Delete this instructor
  2. Save a course in Kursagenten where the instructor is added
Alternative image: An extra image that can be used on the instructor page.

Automatic cleanup: An automatic nightly cleanup also runs at 03:00 every night, so manual cleanup is not always necessary. Use manual cleanup if you have many expired course dates showing on the website and want to clean up immediately.
Automatic creation: Go to Course design → WordPress pages and click "Create page" for each page you need. The shortcode is inserted automatically.
Automatic synchronization via webhooks: When webhooks are configured, courses are updated automatically when they change in Kursagenten. This is the recommended method for ongoing updates.
Automatic: Locations are automatically assigned to regions based on county when regions are enabled.
Blocks coming soon: we are planning modules for course lists with course locations/links and information about upcoming courses.
Build your own design is active. Kursagenten uses the WordPress template hierarchy so your theme/page builder can control the layout.
Build your own design is active. You can still control course data display below.
Cache: Clear cache if you do not see changes immediately.

Checkbox before synchronization: When you click "Fetch all courses from Kursagenten", you can check "Clean up courses after synchronization". This runs cleanup automatically after synchronization is complete. Note: Cleanup takes an extra 3–5 minutes.
Clean and simple list: Based on Standard list, but without background and with fewer course elements.
Compact: A more compact list view with less spacing. No background, and fewer course elements.
Course categories – Contains [kurskategorier] to display all categories.

Course category list [kurskategorier]
[kurskategorier kilde="bilde/ikon" layout="stablet/rad/liste" grid=3 gridtablet=2 gridmobil=1 radavstand="1rem" stil="standard/kort" bildestr="100px" bildeform="avrundet/rund/firkantet/10px" bildeformat="4/3" overskrift="h3" fontmin="13" fontmaks="18" avstand="2em .5em" skygge="ja" vis="hovedkategorier/subkategorier/slug/standard" st=sted/st=ikke-sted utdrag="ja" klasse="min-klasse"]
Course list (course categories only): Three options control how the category affects the course list:

Course list with filter [kursliste]
[kursliste kategori="web" sted="oslo" måned="9" språk="norsk" list_type="grid" filter="topp" knapper="signup_link" bilder="yes" vis="-sluttdato" st=sted/st=ikke-sted klasse="min-klasse"]

Course location list [kurssteder]
[kurssteder layout="stablet/rad/liste" grid=3 gridtablet=2 gridmobil=1 radavstand="1rem" stil="standard/kort" bildestr="100px" bildeform="avrundet/rund/firkantet/10px" bildeformat="4/3" overskrift="h3" fontmin="13px" fontmaks="15px" avstand="2em .5em" skygge="ja" utdrag="ja" vis="standard/alta,oslo,bergen" region="østlandet" stedinfo="ja" klasse="min-klasse"]
Course locations are created automatically when you synchronize courses from Kursagenten. You cannot add course locations manually here.
Course locations – Contains [kurssteder] to display all course locations.
Courses – Contains [kursliste] to display all courses with filters.
Custom content above/below: You can add regular content in the editor above and below the shortcode to write an introduction, add images or other information.
Default – with image and description: Title and description, then image and description, then course list.
Default: A traditional list view with course cards, one course per row/box.
Design changes: If design changes do not appear, you may need to clear cache and update permalinks.
Display type: Choose whether to show main courses (one course per row/box) or all course dates (equivalent to "Course list with filter").
Display type determines whether to show main courses or all course dates.
Element order: The order in which information is displayed (intro text, main content, course dates, instructors, etc.).
Extended description: A longer text with HTML formatting displayed on the category page.
Extended description: A longer text with HTML formatting displayed on the instructor page.
Extended description: A longer text with HTML formatting displayed on the location page.

Extended description: A longer text with rich text formatting (HTML) displayed on the taxonomy page. Here you can describe the category, location or instructor with text, images and other information.
Extra fields: Specific fields depending on the taxonomy type (see below).
Grid: A grid of course cards in multiple columns. You can choose the number of columns for desktop, tablet and mobile.
Hide in auto menus: When enabled, the category is hidden in auto-generated menus that use the [ka-meny] shortcode. Useful if you have categories that should not appear in the main menu.
Hide in overview lists: When enabled, the category is hidden in shortcodes such as [kurskategorier] and similar lists. The category will still be accessible directly via URL.
Icon: An alternative image that can be used instead of the main image in lists. PNG icons work well here (use kilde=ikon in the shortcode).
Image: An image displayed on the location page and in lists.
Image: Upload a main image displayed on the taxonomy page and in lists.
Images: Use placeholder images via shortcodes if you are missing images.
Important: Changes to the design template may require cache refresh and permalink updates, especially for extensive URL customizations.

Important: The course location name in WordPress admin is read-only when it comes from Kursagenten. To change the name shown on the website, use the name change feature instead of editing the taxonomy directly.
Important: When you override fields, they will no longer be updated automatically from Kursagenten. You must update them manually if there are changes in Kursagenten.

Instructor list [instruktorer]
[instruktorer layout="stablet/rad/liste" grid=3 gridtablet=2 gridmobil=1 radavstand="1rem" stil="standard/kort" bildestr="100px" bildeform="avrundet/rund/firkantet/10px" bildeformat="4/3" overskrift="h3" fontmin="13px" fontmaks="15px" avstand="2em .5em" skygge="ja" skjul="Iris,Anna" utdrag="ja" beskrivelse="ja" klasse="min-klasse"]
Instructors – Contains [instruktorer] to display all instructors.
Layout and structure: Where elements are placed on the page (header, content, sidebar, footer).
Layout: Choose the page design. This controls where/how title, text, main image and course list are displayed.

Layout determines the arrangement of elements on the page (header, columns, hooks).
List design determines how courses are displayed in the list (default, grid, plain, compact, date and title).
Display type determines whether to show main courses or all course dates.
List design: Choose list design (default, grid, compact, simple list, simple cards).
List design: Choose the course list design (default, grid, compact, simple cards...)
List design determines how courses are displayed in the list (default, grid, plain, compact, date and title).
List display: In the left column, lists are shown as checkboxes; above the course list, lists are shown as dropdown menus.
Main image: An image displayed on the category page and in lists.

Manual synchronization: Go to Synchronization and click "Fetch all courses from Kursagenten". This should be done the first time you set up the plugin, and when you need to update all courses at once.
Manual: You can change the region for individual locations by editing the course location in the Course locations overview.
NOTE! Enter settings from Kursagenten before fetching all your courses (sync all courses).

Name change for course locations:
You can change course location names under Synchronization → Course location name changes. When you change a location name, slugs (URLs) for courses with this location are also updated.
The old location is not deleted, but is no longer visible on the website.
Name: The category name (comes from Kursagenten, should not be changed).
Name: The instructor's name (can be split into first and last name).
Name: The location name (comes from Kursagenten, but can be changed via the name change feature).
Note: For users to be able to turn the filter on and off, you must drag "Available courses" into "Filters in left column" or "Filters above course list" under Filter settings.
Note: Regions must be enabled for filtering to work. You can also change the region for individual course locations by editing the location in the Course locations overview.
Number per page: Choose how many courses to display per page.
Order: You can also drag filters to change the order they are displayed in.
Override profile from Kursagenten: Enable this to edit name, email and phone. When enabled, these fields will not be updated automatically from Kursagenten during synchronization.
Override profile image: Enable this to use your own uploaded image instead of the image from Kursagenten.

Page builder (Kadence Elements, Elementor, etc.): If you want to control single/archive/taxonomy with page builder conditions, go to Course design → Single course and enable Use WordPress default template hierarchy. The plugin then lets the theme/page builder choose the template without forcing the plugin's own layout files.
Permalinks: When changing URL settings, save "Permalinks" again.
Placement: Choose filters by dragging them to the correct position. You can show them to the left of the course list and/or above the course list.
Profile image: A main image displayed on the instructor page and in lists. This can be from Kursagenten or your own uploaded image.
Profile – around image and title: Around the image, then title and description, then course list.
Region: (If regions are enabled) Choose which region the location belongs to.

Regions:
Regions are enabled. You can manage regional division under Synchronization → Regions. Regional assignment can be changed for each course location.

Regions:
You can enable and manage regions under Synchronization → Regions.
Select existing page: You can also select an existing WordPress page from the dropdown and assign it to a function. Paste the shortcode manually. You can copy the code from the icon next to the name.

Separate cleanup: You can also run cleanup separately by clicking the "Clean up courses" button on the Synchronization page. This is useful if you only want to clean up without re-synchronizing all courses.
Separate design templates: Enable this to choose different design templates for categories, locations and instructors.
Shared design template: All taxonomies use the same design template.
Short description: A short text that can be displayed in lists and overviews. Often shown directly below the heading.
Short description: A short text that can be displayed in lists.
Short description: A short text that can be displayed in lists. Often shown directly below the heading.
Show images: Turn images in the list on/off. Best suited if you have uploaded images for courses in Kursagenten.

Show: The category appears in the filter, and courses tagged with the category appear in the course list.
Hide only the category from filter: The category is hidden from the filter, but courses tagged with this category still appear in the course list (under their other categories).
Hide the category and all related courses: Neither the category nor courses tagged with it appear in the course list – useful for internal categories.
Simple cards: White cards with heading and text. Shows the next available date for courses. You can choose the number of columns for desktop, tablet and mobile.
Simple – title and short description only: Title and short description, then course list. Suitable when you do not have images or extended description.
Slug: URL-friendly version of the name (should not be changed as it may break links).
Slug: URL-friendly version of the name (updated automatically when the name changes).
Synchronization: If courses are not updated automatically, check that webhooks are configured correctly in Kursagenten.
Tags or lists: Filters can be shown as tags or checkboxes. Tags are buttons that select one filter at a time; checkboxes allow selecting multiple filters at once.
Test: Visit the frontend and verify it looks as expected.
Use in shortcodes: You can filter course locations by region in the [kurssteder] shortcode:
Visual details: Styling, spacing and other visual design.
Warning: Name and slug for course locations cannot be changed here. You can change the name under Synchronization.
What are shortcodes? Shortcodes are small codes you place in content (pages/posts) to display dynamic lists and components from the plugin.
When should you use shortcodes? If you build pages with Elementor or other page builders, shortcodes will likely be what you need to use.
Where are they used? Usually on dedicated pages or posts (e.g. "Courses", "Course categories", "Course locations", "Instructors"). You can also use them in menus or widgets.
Width: Choose whether to use the theme's default width or full width. In most cases, default width works as intended.

A payment page is also created automatically when the plugin is activated. This page contains code that generates the payment box and is required for payment functionality to work. The payment page is published automatically, while the other overview pages are created as drafts so you can edit them before publishing.
Add custom CSS class to the wrapper element. Useful for custom styling or theme-specific needs.
Add images, descriptions and organize into main categories
Add payment link %1$s in Kursagenten
Add profile images and extended information
After any course list section on the detail page.
After links in the header section.
After registration you will receive an email with more information about the course and how it will be conducted.
After registration you will receive an email with more information about the course.
All available course locations and dates

All system pages follow WordPress's standard template hierarchy, so if your theme has its own templates (e.g. single-ka_course.php or taxonomy-ka_coursecategory.php), those are used instead of the plugin's default template.

All taxonomy pages (categories, course locations and instructors) can be enriched with extra content such as images, text and descriptions. This lets you customize each page with unique content beyond what comes from Kursagenten.
Also check "Clean up courses" before running sync to ensure old course dates are removed.
Also show manually created instructors without published courses.
An error occurred while fetching filter counts
An external link can be added to the course in the "Link to registration form:" field in Kursagenten. This will open a page of your choice.
An unknown error occurred while filtering.
Applies parent category to all selected course categories.
Applies to filters placed in the left column of the course list.
At the bottom of the right column.
At the top of the right column.
At the very bottom, after the course list (bottom section).
Auto menus are used when you want the menu to automatically show taxonomies and/or courses without manually updating menu items when something changes.
Auto menus in the WordPress menu
Automatic uses responsive srcset when possible for sharper images.
Automatically adds and removes a block of xmlrpc.php in .htaccess when the option is turned on/off. Requires Apache and write access to .htaccess.
Below heading, above filter/pagination and list.
Blocks for single courses will come in a later update. For now, you can build content with your theme/page builder and existing hooks/shortcodes.
Both fields must be filled in

Can be used in course category lists if you want a different image than the main image. Profile image corresponds to icon image in older setups. Use a .png or .svg image file. You can download icons from e.g. https://thenounproject.com/
Can't find what you're looking for?

Category text: we have no fields for this in Kursagenten, and everything must be added on the website. You can add both Short description and Long description. These appear on the individual course category pages on the website.
Changes are saved automatically when you move counties.
Changes page width. Go to Design variables to change max content width.
Changing the design template changes the entire presentation of the course. This can affect how visitors experience the course information.
Check "Clean up courses" if many expired course dates are shown on the website. A nightly cleanup runs, so this may be unnecessary now. NB: Cleanup takes an extra 3-5 minutes.
Check "Enable regional division" to enable the feature.
Check Change URLs if you need to customize URL structures. Here you can change e.g. from "/kurs/your-course" to "/undervisning/your-course".
Choose "Full name", "First name" or "Last name" in the "Name display" field.
Choose a parent to create a hierarchy and make this a subcategory.
Choose a shared design for course categories, course locations and instructors. You can also choose to have separate designs for each taxonomy.
Choose filters on the course list in Course design, and other design choices (list/grid, details, and template selection).
Choose fully square images, slightly rounded corners, or round images.
Choose how course lists on taxonomy pages are displayed:
Choose list design for the [kursliste] shortcode without changing the global setting in Course design.
Choose the desired auto menu (e.g. Course categories, Categories and courses, Course locations or Instructors) and click Add to menu.
Choose the page design. This controls where/how title, text, main image and course list are displayed.
Choose to show first name, last name or both.
Choose to show/hide course categories/menu items belonging to specific locations. Example: show all courses that are not online courses: st="ikke-nettbasert"
Choose whether the course list should show standard button style or registration link style only.
Choose whether the menu should show top-level categories, subcategories or start in a specific category.
Choose whether this should appear in Kursagenten's own overviews.
Choose whether this should appear in auto-generated menus.
Choose whether this should appear in category lists and overviews.
Choose which filters to display on the course list. You can drag the filter to either the left column or above the course list. Choose whether the filter should be displayed as tags or a checklist.
Choose which heading to use for names.
Choose which region this location belongs to.
Cleanup removes courses and course dates that no longer exist in Kursagenten, as well as expired course dates. This keeps the website tidy and up to date.
Click "Change name for new location" to add a new name change.
Click "Save" to save the name change.
Click the triangle on the menu item to open settings. Here you can among other things:
Click to view individual course days with date, time, instructor and room.

Cloudflare is blocking the download with bot verification. URL: %s. Solution: Go to Cloudflare Dashboard > Security > WAF > Custom Rules and add a rule that allows /kursagenten-api/download_plugin, or disable Bot Fight Mode for this URL.
Contact us and we will help you find the perfect course for your needs.

Course categories can be enriched with images, icons and descriptions. You can also control where categories appear.
Course categories, course locations and instructors
Course categories, course locations and instructors can be enriched with text and images if desired. More information in the Enrich taxonomy pages section.
Course date does not exist. coursedate_id must be the WordPress post ID for a ka_coursedate, not Kursagenten's course ID.
Course dates that are deleted or expired
Course list with filter more info
Course locations can be enriched with images and descriptions. You can also change location names and organize them in regions.
Course locations have the same visibility settings as course categories (hide in overview lists and in auto menus).
Courses deleted in Kursagenten (no longer in the API)

Create or select the necessary pages for courses, course categories, course locations and instructors. Choose design for course list, single course, categories, locations and instructors. Choose primary colors.
Custom instructors work the same way as instructors from Kursagenten, but they will not be updated automatically and can be freely edited without enabling "override" features.
Customize basic design variables such as colors, font sizes, and max width on plugin pages.
Customize the course list display. This is shown on the system page "Courses" and everywhere else that uses the shortcode [kursliste].
Default (top filter + left filter as configured)

Default images are used as a fallback to prevent broken designs. These are used as placeholders when an image is missing. If you choose no images, we use Kursagenten's default replacement images if necessary. You can also set placeholder image URLs via shortcodes.
Design templates present content in different ways.

Different layouts. Stacked shows image above course/category name; row shows image to the left. List shows all names stacked vertically. List has less spacing between items and works better with small images or no images.
Displayed right after H1 in the top section.
Displayed right below the entire header block.
Drag counties between regions to organize them according to your needs.

Drag counties between regions to organize them. Note that some counties are outdated but are included for backward compatibility. Use "Reset to default" to reset the regional division to default values.
Drag the auto menu to the correct position in the menu structure. As a main menu item it can be at the top level, or you can drag it under an existing page (e.g. "Courses") to use it as a submenu.
Drag the menu item to where the menu should appear, for example as a submenu under the "Courses" page.

Each category, location and instructor automatically gets its own page. These are generated from templates chosen in Course design. You can enrich these pages with extra content, images and text by editing the taxonomies.

Each course has its own page generated automatically based on the selected design template. Content is fetched from Kursagenten, including the image. You can add custom content between intro text and main content by editing the course. You will see a marker for this area when you visit the page as an administrator, plus a link to edit. There is also a shortcut to edit the course in Kursagenten. Look for a pencil icon you can click. This opens the course in Kursagenten in a new tab. Make your changes there, save, and you will see the changes on the website immediately.
Edit the instructor to add images, contact information and descriptions.
Enable regional division (Sørlandet, Østlandet, Vestlandet, Midt-Norge, Nord-Norge)
Enable the settings you want to use:
Enable this if you use other SEO plugins that are not listed below, or want to handle SEO entirely on your own.
Enter URLs for social profiles. These can be used across the website.
Enter brief information about the company. This text can be displayed with a shortcode.
Enter information that will be used in various places on the website. This includes the main contact name (privacy policy), as well as company name and address (contact page and footer).

Enter information that will be used in various places on the website. This includes the main contact name (privacy policy), as well as company name and address (contact page and footer).
You can also add information about the company that can be displayed with a shortcode. See all shortcodes here.
Enter settings to automatically fetch courses from Kursagenten. Once you have entered the settings, you can click "Get all courses from Kursagenten" to fetch everything on first-time installation.
Enter the image size you want in pixels. For no image, enter 0. When bildestr is set to 0, images are not loaded at all.
Enter the spacing between rows in pixels, em or rem. For no spacing, enter 0.
Exception: You can add extra content between "Intro text" and "Content" on single courses if you want to enrich courses with site-specific information.
Fill in name and optionally slug.
Filter course locations by region (only if regions are enabled). Can be combined with the vis parameter (OR logic: shows locations from the region OR the specified locations).
Filter lists in the left column can become long. They are limited to a default height with a "Show more" link. Default height is 250px. You can choose a different height for filter lists below.
Filter the location list to show only specific locations. You can use location names or slugs (case-insensitive).
Find the "Kursagenten auto menus" box in the left column. If you do not see it, check that it is enabled under "Screen options" at the top.
Find the perfect course for you and your business
First create a custom menu or a menu item of type "Custom link".
For "Categories and courses": choose whether the menu should only show courses (without the category level).
For categories with multiple levels, you can show only top-level categories, only subcategories, only subcategories under a given parent slug, or all.
For course providers with few courses and 1/no categories: shows only a flat list of main courses without category levels.
For instructors from Kursagenten, you can override the data with your own values:
For more examples of menu shortcodes and how to combine them with other shortcodes, see the Shortcodes section further up on this page.

Found a bug or have suggestions for improvement? We'd love to hear from you. We're working to make Kursagenten better for you.
Send an email directly to the inbox in Trello:
Go to Company information and fill in basic company details.

Go to WordPress pages. Edit the page with the instructor overview and add vis="fornavn" or vis="etternavn" to the shortcode to show only first name or last name. You can go directly to editing from WordPress pages.
Go to Synchronization → Course location name changes.
Go to the taxonomy overview (e.g. Course categories, Course locations or Instructors) and click "Edit" on the taxonomy you want to enrich. Here you can add:
Good practice for uploading images is a maximum size of 500 KB. When transferring from Kursagenten, there is a maximum transfer limit of 1 MB.

Grab the filter you want to use and drag it to either the left column or above the course list. Choose whether the filter should be displayed as tags or a checklist.
To remove a filter, drag it back to available filters. Remember to save your changes.
Here you can add custom CSS that will be loaded on all pages belonging to the plugin. This CSS has the highest priority and will override the plugin's default CSS.

Here you can change names for course locations that come from Kursagenten. When you change the name of a location, the slugs (URLs) of courses at that location are also updated.

1. The new location is saved when you click "Save".
2. The location is "translated" to the new name when courses are fetched from Kursagenten.
3. Slugs/URLs for courses at this location are updated immediately.
4. The old location is not deleted, but is no longer visible on the website.

5. Important: To apply the name changes, run a full sync from Kursagenten by clicking "Get all courses from Kursagenten". Also remember to check "Clean up courses" before running the sync.

Here you will find settings for synchronizing courses from Kursagenten.
- By clicking "Get all courses from Kursagenten" you transfer all your courses. Note that synchronization is one-way only.
- We recommend syncing courses automatically. Add Webhooks in Kursagenten so the course is transferred when it is saved/created. See URL below.
- You can also clean up courses if there are outdated dates or similar.
Hide all filters, course counter, "show number of courses" and sorting
Hide instructor by writing a comma-separated list of instructor first names as entered in the "first name" field
Hide location filter in further views
Hide only the category from filter
Hide only the category from filter (courses still shown)
Hide specific locations on course locations
Hide the category and all related courses

How fallback works: When "Hide in URLs" is enabled, we first try a short URL without prefix. If the slug conflicts with a page/post or another hidden taxonomy, a prefix URL is used automatically (e.g. /kurskategori/slug/ or your custom value such as /kat/slug/). Regular WordPress pages and posts always take priority in case of conflict.

Icons are available as HTML with CSS classes. You can use them directly in HTML code. Control size and color with width, height and background-color on i.ka-icon. Remember to add icon- before the icon name. E.g. icon-calendar. Example:
If regions are enabled, you can assign each course location to a region (Sørlandet, Østlandet, Vestlandet, Midt-Norge, Nord-Norge). This can be done in two ways:
If you choose to show an image, it will first look for a main image from the course location. If none is found, a placeholder image is used.

If you create pages for courses, course categories, course locations and/or instructors, the pages are created as regular WordPress pages. You can freely change the title and content. A shortcode is inserted automatically.
Blocks: The taxonomies Course categories, Course locations and Instructors are available as separate Gutenberg blocks in the block editor. You can choose to use the blocks instead of shortcodes if you build the pages with Gutenberg.
If you hide the prefix in URLs, conflicts may occur with regular pages, posts or other taxonomies with the same slug. See information about the fallback mechanism below.

If you only use the shortcodes [kurskategorier], [kurssteder] or [instruktorer], the default values are used.
Alternatively, use the full codes above and remove the options you do not need.

Images are fetched from the instructor profile in Kursagenten. This image can be overridden on the website. You can also use an alternative image if you want a different style or image than the one used in Kursagenten.
Images can be added to each individual course in Kursagenten. If no image has been uploaded, a placeholder image can be used.

Important info about URLs
Here you can change the URL for courses, instructor, course category and course location. NOTE! Do not change unless you know what you are doing. This can break the site and make these pages unavailable. Remember to save permalink settings after making a change.
Important: Courses should not be edited on the website in general – all editing should be done in Kursagenten. This ensures the information is up to date and correctly synchronized.
In Course design you can choose design templates and settings for taxonomy pages (categories, course locations and instructors).
In Course design → Single course you choose the design template for single course pages. The design template affects the entire layout of the course detail page:
In Link text (or equivalent field), paste the desired [ka-meny] shortcode, e.g. [ka-meny type="kurskategorier"].
In WordPress there are two types of pages: Pages and posts. Posts correspond to blog posts or other content with categories and tags. Enable to rename posts to "Articles".
In the URL field, type # (so the menu item can be saved without an actual link).

Insert the shortcode in the text field of a custom menu. In the URL field, type #. To get the menu as a submenu, drag this menu item to the right under another menu item. To create a menu from a specific category (with subcategories), enter the category slug after start="".
Instructors can be enriched with images, contact information and descriptions. You can also create custom instructors that do not come from Kursagenten, and override data from Kursagenten.

Instructors have the same visibility settings as course categories (hide in overview lists and in auto menus). You can also hide specific instructors in shortcodes using the skjul parameter in the [instruktorer] shortcode.
Just below main image/extended description, before the course list.
Keep H3 as default unless you have a clear semantic reason to change it.

Kursagenten adds meta tags, Open Graph, Twitter Cards and Course schema on course and taxonomy pages. When you have an SEO plugin installed, we adapt automatically to avoid duplicates.
You can also turn off our SEO entirely if you use other solutions.
Kursagenten offers both auto menus and menu shortcodes, so you can build dynamic menus that always reflect current course categories, course locations and instructors.

List and grid design more info
List design can be changed with attributes in the shortcode. See the overview for available options.
Location descriptions from the 'Location' field in Kursagenten.

Location names are fetched from Kursagenten. You can change the location name. For this to be correct in all titles and URLs, do this under Admin → Kursagenten → Synchronization. Change the name, then re-fetch all courses. All occurrences of the location name will then display correctly.

Location text: we have no fields for this in Kursagenten, and everything must be added on the website. You can add both Short description and Long description. These appear on the individual course location pages on the website.
Log in to Kursagenten and go to Integration settings → Webhooks.
Main image used on the category page and in category overviews
Menu shortcodes are used when you want to build menus using the [ka-meny] shortcode, typically in a custom menu or in content where the theme supports shortcodes.
Missing Kursagenten IDs on course date.
More information about data flow from Kursagenten

Most data should be managed from Kursagenten. For a few parts we do not have fields, and for those you can enrich category/location/instructor directly on the website via Admin → Kursagenten → Course locations/Instructors/Course locations.
NB! Run a full sync from Kursagenten immediately after adding/changing the name of a location(s)!
Name change saved. Slug updates will occur at next synchronization.
Name change updated. Slug updates will occur at next synchronization.
Name, phone number, and email are transferred from Kursagenten. This can be overridden on the website if desired.

Normally these areas are slightly darker than the background. Also used as the primary color on boxes in the "Boxes" design.

Note: AIOSEO (Pro), SEOPress (Pro) and Slim SEO Schema (separate extension) can also set up their own Course schema. If you enable this in those extensions, we recommend turning off Kursagenten SEO to avoid duplicates. For Rank Math Free we still recommend Kursagenten's Course schema as the default.

Note: on the page with the instructor overview, you must add vis="fornavn" or vis="etternavn" to the shortcode to show first name or last name only. You can go directly to editing from WordPress pages.
Note: the instructor must be linked to a course for the data to be fetched.
One card per course and location (next date per location)
Only for positive filter (e.g. online): hides the location filter on the course list page. With a "not-" filter, the filter is shown so the user can choose another location.

Open Synchronization and connect to your Kursagenten account. Add Webhooks in Kursagenten so the course is transferred automatically when saved/created.
The first time, click "Fetch all courses from Kursagenten" to fetch all courses at once.
Override filter display for [kursliste]. Useful when the same list is used in different page layouts.
Override which details are shown for each course in [kursliste]. Without a prefix, only specified fields are shown. With -/! fields are hidden; with + fields are shown without changing the rest.
Overrides the text color from the overlay selection above.

Pages are marked with "Kursagenten" in the page overview. By clicking you copy the shortcode to the clipboard and can paste it where you want to display it on an existing page.

Regions let you organize course locations into geographic areas (Sørlandet, Østlandet, Vestlandet, Midt-Norge, Nord-Norge). This is useful for filtering and organizing course locations.

Shortcodes can be inserted in page and post content. You can insert the full course list, or lists of all course categories, courses in the same category (used on course pages), or instructors.
There are many options. You will find the full shortcode below with all options, plus a list explaining every option.
Copy the shortcode and paste it where you want to display it.
Note that you must remove options you do not need, and parts where multiple options are listed (e.g. stablet/rad/liste).

Show filter with categories also used on courses with this category, in addition to subcategories. Useful if you cannot create a parent category with subcategories but still want filtering.

Test frontend: course list, single course, categories, course locations and instructors, and adjust filters/design as needed.
Text adjusts to screen size. This is the smallest font size you will use for text and headings.

Text is fetched from the intro text and course description in Kursagenten. You can add your own text on the website as well. Navigate to the course while logged in as admin, then click "Add extra WordPress content" between the intro text and course description.

The category name is fetched from tags in Kursagenten. You can structure these in your desired hierarchy on the website, up to two levels. If you want to change the category name, you should change the tag in Kursagenten. Note: a new course category will be created, but the old one will not be deleted. Transfer any text and images to the new category.
The changes below apply to all selected terms. Fields set to "Do not change" are skipped.
The course is full. You can register your interest for more information or to join the waiting list.

The course list can be filtered by category, location, start month, language, etc. Filters are part of the [kursliste] shortcode. If you created pages via Course design → WordPress pages, this applies to the "Courses" page.
The entire filter area in the left column is placed in a rounded box. Default background follows the theme color (--ka-box-background).
The name as displayed on the page. Can be changed under Synchronization.
The name as displayed on the page. Should only be changed on the instructor's user profile in Kursagenten.
The name is what is displayed on the page
The old location is not deleted, but it will no longer be visible on the website.
The plugin automatically creates system pages for single pages handled by the plugin. These pages are generated dynamically and do not need to be created manually:
The search field gets a pill shape, clear border and magnifying glass on the right. Only applies when search is in the left column.

The taxonomies Course categories, Course locations and Instructors are available as separate Gutenberg blocks in the block editor.

There are not many design templates available for single courses yet. We plan to add more design templates soon.
There are two ways to clean up:
These settings can be set when editing the category, via quick edit, and via bulk edit ("Kursagenten: Bulk edit visibility") in the overview.
These settings work as defaults for shortcodes. You can also override them directly in the shortcode with attributes. See Shortcodes for lists and grid for more information.
These slugs use fallback with prefix.
This adds rating to the Course schema on course pages. It also adds a redirect based on query strings in emails from Kursagenten to the rating form on the correct course page.
This field is overridden from Kursagenten
This field shows whether the image was automatically fetched from Kursagenten.
This image can be used as an alternative image on the instructor profile.
This image is used as the main image on the instructor profile.
This is the largest font size you will use for text and headings.

This option adds:
1) Clickjacking Protection (X-Frame-Options) in WordPress, strengthens security headers.
2) Disables theme and plugin editing
3) Removes WP version number
4) Disables XML-RPC in WordPress
This option disables Gravatar on the frontend and uses a local default image instead. This can resolve issues with tracking prevention in some browsers.
This page helps you get started quickly with setting up the Kursagenten plugin from A to Z. Follow the steps in order, and use the left menu to jump between sections.
This view uses the simpler list with the next available date and does not have the same filter options as "Show all course dates".
Tip: If you have many course categories, you can select some as main categories or create your own. This makes the categories more manageable for visitors.

To display overviews and lists of courses, categories, locations and instructors, you must create regular WordPress pages with shortcodes. These pages should be created from Course design → WordPress pages. If you already have pages you want to use, select them from the dropdown.
Try changing your filter choices or reset all filters to see all available courses.
Turn off SEO for courses and taxonomy pages
Under Course design you control list design on system pages. There are settings for image use, number of columns and pagination.
Under Course design → WordPress pages you can create the following pages:
Use "Reset to default" to restore the regional division to default values.
Use course image, and placeholder image for course if course image is missing
Use course image, and background color if course image is missing
Use default selection for selected list design
Use external link on course title
Use external link on registration button
Use taxonomy image, and general placeholder image if image is missing
Use taxonomy image, and background color if image is missing
Use the type, start and optionally st parameters to control which categories/locations or instructors are displayed.
Used to highlight certain boxes in the "Boxes" design. If only "Background for highlighted areas" is set, both get the same color.
Used when Shadow/border style = None, but use border.
View overview of all imported courses
Webhooks ensure your courses are always up to date without manual effort. To enable webhooks:
What Kursagenten adds (when no SEO plugin is active)
When enabled, locations can be organized into regions. This does not affect synchronization but can be used for filtering and organization.
When these are active, we turn off our meta tags and leave it to the plugin. Course schema is normally provided by Kursagenten, except for Rank Math Pro where we let Rank Math handle Course schema.
When this is enabled, the course list will by default hide full courses and courses "on request". The user can turn the filter off again on the page.
When webhooks are enabled, courses are updated automatically in WordPress when they are changed or created in Kursagenten. This applies to course data, dates and locations.
When you change a location name:

When you visit a course on the frontend, you will find an edit icon that takes you directly to course editing in Kursagenten. This makes it easy to update course information without going through the website.
Whether the image should be landscape, portrait or square.
WordPress generally supports the JavaScript library jQuery. If there is no support for this, you can choose to enable it here.
Works especially well for pages with few courses per location/category/instructor.
You can add extra text above or below the shortcode for introduction, SEO text or other information.
You can add menu items that automatically generate course categories, course locations and instructors. More information in the Menus section.
You can add placeholder images under Admin → Kursagenten → Course design.
You can also configure how course lists on taxonomy pages are displayed. See Control course list appearance for more information.
You can change the name of course locations that come from Kursagenten. This is done via Synchronization → Course location name changes. See Location names and regions for more information.
You can change the name of course locations that come from Kursagenten. This is useful if you want shorter or more descriptive names on the website.
You can choose a shared design template for all taxonomies, or enable separate design templates for each taxonomy type:

You can choose to display full name, first name or last name on the instructor page and in the instructor list. You must change settings in both Course design and the WordPress page that displays the instructors.
You can choose whether to use an image you upload yourself, or the image fetched from Kursagenten. Choose kilde=ka-bilde if you want to use the image from Kursagenten.
You can choose whether to use the main image, or upload your own icons for lists. Choose kilde=ikon if you want to use these.
You can control where categories appear with several visibility settings:
You can create instructors directly in WordPress that do not come from Kursagenten:

You can find the settings for Provider ID and Provider GUID in Kursagenten under Company information -> Settings, and Theme for course lists under Embedded / iframe

In Integration settings, under the Webhooks tab, enter %s in the CourseCreated and CourseUpdated fields to automatically update courses when they are created or changed in Kursagenten.
You can freely change title, slug and content on the pages. The only thing that should not be removed is the shortcode.
You can register for the course now. After registration you will receive more information.

You can upload images for each category. You can also upload a "profile image". This can be used in blocks/shortcodes as an alternative to the main image. If no category image has been uploaded, it will try to use an image from a linked course. If none is found, a placeholder image will be used.
You can use blocks instead of shortcodes if you build pages with Gutenberg. The same options described under Shortcodes for lists and grid are largely available directly in block settings.
You do not have access to this function.
You do not have permission to open this page.
You must enter a license key before you can use the settings.
You must run a full synchronization ("Fetch all courses from Kursagenten") to apply name changes.

Your courses are automatically transferred from Kursagenten along with course categories, course locations and instructors. But to give your website a professional look, you can enrich these with images and text directly on the website.
Your courses are fetched from Kursagenten in two ways:

e.g. h3, h4, h5, p, span, div
Default: h3

first name, last name
Default: shows full name

left, top, filter button, hide
Default: as filters are set up in Course design

show_buttons, signup_link
Default: fetched from Course design
shows all locations in Østlandet or Bergen (OR logic)
shows only course locations in Østlandet

standard, comma-separated list (e.g. alta,oslo,bergen or "Oslo,Mo i Rana")
Default: shows all locations

standard, grid, compact, plain, date-and-title, simple-cards
Default: fetched from Course design

sørlandet, østlandet, vestlandet, midt-norge, nord-norge
Default: no filtering

time, duration, price, location, free-text location, room, instructor, end date, registration deadline
Default: fetched from Course design

top-level categories, subcategories, parent slug (e.g. dance or truck)
Default: shows all

yes, no
Default: fetched from Course design
